sanskrit:
सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत्।
सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः॥
transliteration:
saha-jaṃ karma kaunteya sa-doṣam api na tyajet |
sarvārambhā hi doṣeṇa dhūmenāgnir ivāvṛtāḥ ||
literal_meaning: One should never abandon the work born with oneself, O son of Kuntī, though it be faulty; for all undertakings are surrounded by defect, just as fire is surrounded by smoke.
contextual_meaning: Every life-role carries unavoidable imperfections; abandoning your innate duty because of its flaws only exchanges one set of problems for another. Stay, act, and sanctify the role through mindful, values-aligned engagement rather than avoidance.
psychological_state: Perfectionistic paralysis—fear that any action will cause harm, leading to chronic procrastination or role abandonment.
trigger_scenario: A manager feels ethically conflicted about layoffs but realizes that avoiding the decision only transfers suffering elsewhere; staying present and minimizing harm becomes the higher duty.
anchor_text: Stay in your role, flaws and all, and serve with mindful presence.
